channel.title: Libertas - Freedom Tech Signals
channel.description: Curated insights on freedom technology: Bitcoin, privacy tools, censorship resistance, and digital sovereignty.

item.title: EFF Closes in Solidarity with National Shutdown Against ICE/CBP Actions
item.description: Electronic Frontier Foundation is closing Friday, Jan 30 to join national shutdown opposing ICE/CBP enforcement, while providing protest security resources.

item.title: Microsoft Provides FBI BitLocker Decryption Keys ~20 Times Annually
item.description: Microsoft gives FBI access to BitLocker encryption keys about 20 times per year via court orders when users store keys on Microsoft's servers for convenience.

item.title: Senate Democrats Propose Ban on ICE/CBP Facial Recognition Use
item.description: New Senate bill would prohibit ICE and CBP from using facial recognition and other biometric surveillance, requiring deletion of existing data and allowing civil suits for violations.

item.title: Russian Spy Satellites Reportedly Intercept EU Communications Infrastructure
item.description: European officials believe Russian space vehicles have intercepted communications from dozens of EU satellites, potentially compromising sensitive data and enabling trajectory manipulation.

item.title: Tails 7.4.1 Emergency Release Fixes Critical OpenSSL Vulnerabilities in Tor
item.description: Tails released emergency update fixing critical OpenSSL vulnerabilities that could allow malicious Tor relays to deanonymize users.

item.title: Cryptography Expert Analyzes WhatsApp Backdoor Lawsuit Claims
item.description: Class action lawsuit alleges WhatsApp can read all encrypted messages despite E2E claims. Cryptography expert Matthew Green examines technical feasibility and evidence quality of backdoor allegations.

item.title: Gabon Imposes Nation-Scale Internet Blackout During Presidential Elections
item.description: Gabon cut internet access nationwide during August 2023 elections, citing violence prevention. Service was restored after military coup ended the president's rule, highlighting critical need for censorship-resistant communications.

item.title: OONI Announces Anonymous Credential System for Censorship Measurement
item.description: OONI is implementing anonymous credentials to authenticate probe measurements without revealing user identity or enabling cross-network tracking of censorship researchers.

item.title: Silent.Link: Bitcoin-Native eSIM Service Bypasses SIM-Swap Attacks and State Censorship
item.description: Silent.Link offers privacy-focused eSIM data/SMS services paid with Bitcoin, eliminating SIM-swap risks while bypassing state firewalls in China and Dubai's VoIP restrictions.

item.title: HRW Reports Systematic Rights Abuses Across Southern Africa in 2025
item.description: Human Rights Watch documents widespread government crackdowns on protesters, journalists, and activists across Southern Africa in 2025, with security forces using lethal force and arbitrary detention while regional bodies fail to intervene.

item.title: Kimwolf Botnet Sybil Attack Disrupts I2P Anonymity Network
item.description: The massive Kimwolf IoT botnet accidentally crippled I2P's anonymous communications network by flooding it with 700k infected devices attempting to use it as fallback C2 infrastructure.

item.title: New York Proposes Mandatory 3D Printer Surveillance for Firearm Detection
item.description: New York's budget bill would require all 3D printers sold in the state to include 'blocking technology' that scans files for potential firearm blueprints and refuses to print flagged items.

item.title: Bangladesh's 2026 Election Tests Digital Democracy After Youth-Led Uprising
item.description: Bangladesh conducts major election 18 months after youth uprising toppled autocratic regime, but complex referendum structure, forced digitization, and low women's representation reveal democratic transition challenges.
